notes: v1.14.122 - UI consistency sweep RULE A (one back control per surface), pass 1. The legacy header chevron (#nav-back-btn, nav_back/history.back) was showing in the New UI ON TOP OF the redesign's own back controls - measured 3 stacked backs on the scope picker + deploy detail (chevron + per-page #work-back + inline '< Back' pill) and 2 on Ref panels (chevron + #ref-back). Per Rule A's prescription ('a sub-flow hides the header chevron rather than stacking a second one'), hide the chevron in the New UI: body.rd #nav-back-btn{display:none!important} (!important beats the inline display the legacy nav code toggles). The redesign already provides a back on every surface - #work-back/#ref-back (grid-back) on panels, the inline pill in deploy/rack/sop detail - so nothing is stranded (browser-verified: chevron gone everywhere, every drilled surface keeps a working back, #work-back returns to grid). Resolves BOTH listed Rule A violations: scope-picker 'two app-backs at once' and deploy-detail 'chevron jammed against/overlapping the header band'. Optics/Command Center/simple panels are now exactly ONE back. CSS-only, redesign-only; legacy (?legacy=1) keeps the chevron, byte-identical. RESIDUAL (flagged for John, not fixed here - a nav-model taste call): deploy/rack/sop DETAIL still carry 2 backs with different semantics - #work-back (jump to grid) + inline '< Back' (step back one level); and .rf-back-btn is 40px vs Rule A's >=56px target. DEVICE-VERIFY: no header chevron in the New UI; every screen still has its own back; ?legacy=1 chevron unchanged.
